The term “eligible device” means a powered mobility device that does not meet the requirements for sale contained in § 20-610 of the Administrative Code, a motorized scooter as defined in subdivision a of § 19-176.2 of the Administrative Code, or a limited use motorcycle as defined in § 121-b of the New York state vehicle and traffic law. [ALP S-102]
Food delivery worker. The term “food delivery worker” has the meaning ascribed to such term by § 20-1501 of the Administrative Code. [ALP S-102]
Food service establishment. The term “food service establishment” has the meaning ascribed to such term by § 20-1501 of the Administrative Code. [ALP S-102]
Operable condition. The term “operable condition” means a powered mobility device, motorized scooter, or limited use motorcycle that performs its intended function. [ALP S-102]

Powered bicycle. The term “powered bicycle” means a class one bicycle with electric assist or a class two bicycle with electric assist, as such terms are defined in § 102-c of the New York state vehicle and traffic law. [ALP S-102]
Powered mobility device. The term “powered mobility device” means a powered bicycle or an electric scooter as defined in § 114-e of the New York state vehicle and traffic law. [ALP S-102]
Third-party courier service. The term “third-party courier service” has the meaning ascribed to such term by § 20-1501 of the Administrative Code. [ALP S-102]
Third-party food delivery service. The term “third-party food delivery service” has the meaning ascribed to such term by § 20-1501 of the Administrative Code. [ALP S-102]
Trade-in device. The term “trade-in device” means a powered mobility device that meets the requirements for sale contained in § 20-610 of the Administrative Code. [ALP S-102]
Trade-in event. The term “trade-in event” means a specific date, time and location where an applicant delivers an eligible device in exchange for a trade-in device. [ALP S-102]

Trade-in program. The term “trade-in program” means an initiative whereby eligible applicants receive up to one trade-in device from the City of New York at no cost in exchange for an eligible device. [ALP S-102]
(b) Eligibility. An applicant must: [ALP S-102]
(1)Earn at least $1,500, over the 12 months preceding the application submission period, as a food delivery worker at: [ALP S-102]
i.a food service establishment; [ALP S-102]
ii.a third-party food delivery service; or [ALP S-102]
iii.a third-party courier service. [ALP S-102]
(2)Own an eligible device that is in operable condition; [ALP S-102]
(3)Be at least 18 years of age; and [ALP S-102]
(4)Reside in New York City. [ALP S-102]
(c) Application process. An applicant must submit the following information on forms prescribed by the Department: [ALP S-102]
(1)Applicant’s name, address, date of birth, telephone number, and email address; [ALP S-102]

(2)Name(s) of the food service establishment, third-party food delivery service, or third-party courier service where the applicant currently works or has activel worked as a food delivery worker within the 12 months preceding the application submission period; [ALP S-102]
(3)A statement agreeing to abide by the terms and conditions of the trade-in program; and [ALP S-102]
(4)Any other information as specified in the application. [ALP S-102]
(d) Acceptable forms of proof. An applicant must submit proof of the following information: [ALP S-102]
(1)Applicant’s name and date of birth, by providing to the Department a copy of one of the following unexpired documents: [ALP S-102]
i.IDNYC card; [ALP S-102]
ii.Passport; [ALP S-102]
iii.NYS Benefit ID; or [ALP S-102]
iv.U.S. government-issued ID. [ALP S-102]
(2)Residence, by providing to the Department a copy of one of the following unexpired documents that shows the applicant’s address: [ALP S-102]
i.IDNYC card; [ALP S-102]
ii.U.S. government-issued ID; [ALP S-102]
iii.Utility bill, dated within the past 90 days; [ALP S-102]
iv.Telephone bill, dated within the past 90 days; [ALP S-102]
v.Any invoice mailed directly from billing company, dated within the past 90 days; or [ALP S-102]
vi.Any other document(s) approved by the Department. [ALP S-102]
(3)Employment and earnings during the 12 months preceding the application submission period by providing to the Department a copy of one of the following: [ALP S-102]
i.Tax returns including a W-2, 1099-NEC, or 1040 form from a food service establishment, a third-party food delivery service, or a third-party courier service; [ALP S-102]
ii.Bank statements showing direct deposits from a food service establishment, a third-party food delivery service, or a third-party courier service; [ALP S-102]
iii.Pay stubs from a food service establishment, a third-party food delivery service, or a third-party courier service; [ALP S-102]
iv.A letter from the employer confirming employment and earnings; or [ALP S-102]
v.Any other document(s) approved by the Department. [ALP S-102]
(4)Eligible device by providing to the Department all of the following: [ALP S-102]
i.Photos showing the front, side/profile, and back of the eligible device; [ALP S-102]
ii.Photos of the eligible device’s battery; and [ALP S-102]
iii.Photos of the eligible device’s spare or extra battery, if applicable. [ALP S-102]
(5)Proof of ownership by providing to the Department an attestation of ownership of the eligible device in a form prescribed by the Department. [ALP S-102]
(e) Conditions of trade-in program participation. In exchange for a trade-in device, an applicant must: [ALP S-102]
(1)Indemnify the City of New York against legal liabilities associated with the receipt, operation, or use of the trade-in device; [ALP S-102]
(2)Comply with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations related to the operation of the trade-in device, including but not limited to § 10-157 of the Administrative Code; [ALP S-102]
(3)Not resell the trade-in device for a period of one year after receipt of such device; [ALP S-102]
(4)Complete a semiannual survey about the trade-in device and trade-in program by the Department; [ALP S-102]
(5)Deliver the eligible device to the Department at a trade-in event for disposal; [ALP S-102]
(6)If applicable, deliver all batteries used with the eligible device; [ALP S-102]
(7)Demonstrate the eligible device is in operable condition; [ALP S-102]
(8)Not modify the trade-in device in any way; [ALP S-102]
(9)Service the trade-in device safely at a repair shop; and [ALP S-102]
(10)Complete a safety training course. [ALP S-102]
(f) Submission period. An applicant must submit the application within a time period prescribed by the Department on its website. [ALP S-102]

(g) Assignment of trade-in devices. At the conclusion of the submission period, the Department will review all applications and notify eligible applicants of the trade-in events. In the event that there are more eligible applicants than available trade-in devices, the Department will conduct a lottery to determine which eligible applicants will receive trade-in devices. Eligible applicants not selected for trade-in will be placed in a waitlist in numerical order. If a selected applicant does not attend their trade-in appointment or becomes ineligible, their trade-in opportunity will be assigned to the next eligible applicant on the waitlist. [ALP S-102]

(h) Material misrepresentations, misstatements and omissions. The Department may deny an application if the application is found to contain material misrepresentations, misstatements, or omissions. [ALP S-102]
(Added City Record 10/23/2024, eff. 11/22/2024) [ALP S-102]
